text: Modern web development requires careful attention to security. First, HTTPS encryption protects data transmitted between users and websites, preventing unauthorized access. Second, robust authentication systems, like strong passwords and two-factor verification, ensure that only legitimate users can access accounts. Third, input validation is crucial; all user inputs must be screened and sanitized to block malicious code injections. Developers must also guard against common vulnerabilities, such as cross-site scripting (XSS) and SQL injection, which can compromise entire databases. By implementing these practices, developers build safer, more reliable web applications that protect both user data and system integrity.
